sections.title: Before talking to Captain Pudi with level 35 Fishing

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, my name is [player name]. What's wi...

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: I not be in the mood t' talk, sorry. If ye need anythin', talk to Cap'ain Pudi over by the dock.

sections.title: After talking to Captain Pudi with level 35 Fishing

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: I cannot believe that beast ruined me ship.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened to your ship?

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: Was awful, me crew an' I were out on our fortnightly fishin' excursion. We set sail from Al Kharid t' the deep-sea fishin' spot we usually use.

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: Was nothin' but smooth sailin' all the way, we got t' the spot an' started fishin'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like a normal day of fishing to me.

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: Aye, I s'pose it was. After a few days, the crew an' I sailed further t' the Karamjan coast t' catch some sharks. More plunder from sellin' sharks.

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: While sailin', I noticed there was more debris than usual. Koanee said there must have been a storm recently.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did she know that?

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: Koanee is in charge of plottin' a course t' our destination an' watchin' the weather. I thought she was good at her job.

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: We got to our final fishin' spot an' Lauretta let down the net. Almost a whole day went by an' we still 'ad not caught any fish.

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: In fact, I could not see any fish in the waters at all. Such a strange sight.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not even bass?

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: Nothin'. We decided t' pack it in for the day an' I told Koanee t' leave the nets down, hoping we would catch some fish overnight.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That sounds like a bad idea. What would have happened if your net got too full or your ship started drifting and the net got caught on some rocks?

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: 'Twas a risk we 'ad to take t' catch some fish.

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: The next day Koanee woke me up t' tell me a storm was on the horizon, so we lifted the anchor up an' adjusted our course.

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: Not sure what she was playing at with that decision, but out o' nowhere winds started t' change an' the storm was headin' in our direction, we couldn't outrun it.

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: 'Twas also too dangerous to anchor down, so we 'ad to weather it.

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: Thick fog rolled in, giant waves crashin' against the ship's hull. So much lightnin', it felt like Saradomin was angry with us.

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: The lightnin' struck our sails, settin' them ablaze despite the 'eavy rain!

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: The last thing I see is glowin' fish an' a monster in the distance with a twisted face. Next thing I know, I'm lying on the shore just south of Al Kharid with Pudi waving a kebab in me face!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That must have been awful. At least you and the crew survived.

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: Aye, we survived, but the same cannot be said for me poor ship! Koanee should 'o known better!

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: Once Pudi and the others helped us back on our feet, the Chancellor sent us 'ere t' setup shop and recruit others.

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: Not really my area of expertise, mind, but Pudi sorts most of it out!

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: Anythin' else I can help ye with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Got any tips for fighting Tempoross?

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: Always carry an 'ammer. That beast has a habit of destroyin' ships. You can fix it up by using said 'ammer.

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: The Spirit Anglers' ships you fight the beast from are kitted out with everythin' ye need.

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: Can I help ye with anythin' else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found your ship's log.

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: Ye be stealin' me booty are ye?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I jus...

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: I be yankin' ye chain, 'tis all good. I put that 'ere book on that shelf so I may spread me tale.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was a good tale. Is it true?

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: Of course 'tis true! Ye can ask me all about it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have to go.

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: Be seein' ye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would you care for an egg in this difficult time?

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: Um... no, I don't want your damp egg, thanks...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll take my eggs elsewhere then.

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: A damp egg? No thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll take my eggs elsewhere then.

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: I'd rather not. Thanks, though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll take my eggs elsewhere then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would you care for an egg in this difficult time?

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: Oh, thanks.

lines.speaker: Taiman
lines.text: This is some comfort to be sure.
